FAQs | NUST (General FAQs)

Frequently Asked Questions Are there any quota / reserved seats? There are no quota seats. Are there any reserved / quota seats for admission in MBBS Programme? Admissions in NSHS will be purely on merit, based upon the

Mdcat

conducted by
NUMS. Can a candidate apply both for open merit and ACT based seats? Yes, on the basis of NET / MDCAT Score and required ACT Score. Can a candidate of ICS / equivalent qualification eligible to apply for Engineering programmes? Yes, as per PEC regulation No PEC/EAD/46-VCCM/Regulations/2023 dated 30 March 2023, candidates having combination of
Physics, Mathematics
and
Computer Studies / Computer Science (ICS) or equivalent are eligible to apply for all the Engineering programs
offered by NUST, but they have to clear Chemistry as a remedial subject / course in the 1st Semester after admission.

Mdcat

Candidates, who already appeared in any NET series during current admission cycle, will also be able to update their choice preferences while applying for Series-4 or after the announcement of merit, before start of the selection process. Can a candidate with FSc Pre-Medical / equivalent qualification, appeared in additional mathematics paper and waiting for the result, apply for admission in Engineering programs on the basis of Pre Medical marks? Candidate can apply on the basis of Pre-Medical result, however he / she has to provide the additional math result with passing marks and overall 60% marks in HSSC at the time of joining NUST. Can candidates of FSc Pre-Medical / equivalent qualification apply for BS Computer Science?

Mdcat

Yes, but they have to clear deficient Mathematics courses of 6 credit hours, within one year of enrollment in the programme. Can foreign / international students apply for admission in BSHND programme? Foreign students are eligible to apply on the basis of NET-Applied Sciences / NUMS-MDCAT / National MDCAT / ACT Test. Can foreign students apply for admission at NSHS? Foreign students can apply to MBBS Programme at NSHS on the basis of NUMS MDCAT, SAT-II or foreign MCAT / UCAT score. Can I apply for computing programmes with HSSC / A level background in Arts / Humanities group?

Mdcat

Yes, any candidate who has
Mathematics
as one of the mandatory subjects in HSSC / A level / Equivalent, is eligible to apply for computing programmes. Can I apply for more than one programme? Yes, if you meet the eligibility criteria. Can I apply for rechecking of Entry Test Result? Re-checking of
Paper Based
Entry Test may be requested within
5 days
of the declaration of result along with a fee of
Rs.500/-
in the form of a bank draft/pay order in favour of NUST. The
original bank draft / pay order
should be
dispatched
to Admissions Directorate, NUST, Sector H-12, Islamabad, along with written application.

Mdcat

Re-checking involves the verification of paper for any unmarked answers and error free totaling of marks. Can I get a copy of NUST Entry Test result? Desirous candidates may obtain the result details of their Test from the link available on NUST website. Can I get some sample Test papers of NUST Entry Test? Mock Test for Computer Based / Paper Based Test are available on NUST website
www.ugadmissions.nust.edu.pk
under the link
Sample Test, for candidates to practice. Do I fall under the Expatriate Students category? If you are temporarily or permanently residing in a country other than Pakistan, and hold either a Pakistani passport, or dual nationality (one of them being Pakistani), you fall under the Expatriate Student category.

Mdcat

For details, please
click here. Does NUST cater for the pick and drop facility for the students? Pick and drop service is provided to students of Rawalpindi and Islamabad only with specified routes,
for which the student has to pay separate charges. You will be guided to avail the facility during the orientation session. Does NUST cater for the pick and drop facility? Facility is available for the residents of
Rawalpindi
and
Islamabad
with specified routes,
for which they have to pay separate charges. Details will be shared during the orientation session. Does NUST offer scholarship / financial assistance? Information regarding scholarships offered by NUST can be accessed by
clicking here.

Mdcat

Students showing good academic performance by maintaining a minimum GPA of 3.5 out of 4.0 in each semester, are eligible to get financial assistance, as per policy. For various academics backgrounds, which options for various UG disciplines are available? The options available to candidates for admission in various UG programmes according to their academic background are accessible by visiting following link:-
https://nust.edu.pk/admissions/undergraduates/academic-qualification-required-for-different-ug-progammes/
How can I apply at NUST if I am an expatriate student? An expatriate having dual nationality can opt for both NET and SAT on national and international seats. How can I apply for admission in BS Human Nutrition and Dietetics (BSHND)?

Mdcat

To apply online for admission in BSHND, please click on the following link:
http://nshsadmission.nust.edu.pk/hnd/
How can I apply for admission in MBBS Programme? For information regarding MBBS admission in NSHS, please click the following link:
https://nust.edu.pk/admissions/NSHS/how-to-apply/
How can I submit the application processing fee (online) using 1Link option? Candidates can use the unique 17-digit
1Bill Invoice / Consumer No. mentioned under the
Fee Invoice section, to pay their fee via 1Link service through any of the following payment modes:
Mobile App / Online Banking (Any Bank)
ATM (Any Bank)
Easy Paisa / Jazz Cash etc. The benefit of making payment through 1Link is that you don t have to wait for fee verification because it is verified instantly.

Mdcat

How can I submit the application processing fee? Candidates can submit the fee through any of the following options:-
1Link
(1Bill Invoice Number)
Credit Card
How many allied programmes does NSHS offer? Currently, NSHS is offering admission to BS Human Nutrition and Dietetics (BSHND). I am a born Pakistani with a foreign passport; can I apply as an expatriate student? If you don't have Pakistani nationality like having CNIC / NICOP, you can apply as an international student (foreign student). You can apply on the basis of NET or ACT/SAT or both as per eligibility criteria but have to pay tuition fee and allied charges as admissible for foreign nationals.

Mdcat

If you are a POC holder and applying on the basis of NET and ACT / SAT national seat, you will be paying tuition fee and allied charges as admissible for national student. I am a foreigner and want to get admission at NUST? If you fulfill the eligibility criteria, you can apply for admission on the basis of NET or ACT / SAT (International seat) or both as per policy in vogue. For details, visit
https://nust.edu.pk/admissions/undergraduates/international-students/. I am an overseas Pakistani who has completed O & A level from abroad, do I have to pass Urdu, Islamiyat / Ethics, and Pakistan Studies to get IBCC equivalence?

Mdcat

No, there isn t any such compulsory requirement of passing the above-mentioned subjects, as per IBCC equivalence rules. For further details, please visit IBCC website:
https://ibcc.edu.pk/rules-for-equivalence/
I am in my final year of A Level and my result will be announced in August 2024. Can I apply? Yes, you can apply, as in such case you will be evaluated on the basis of O levels equivalence marks but you have to provide equivalence certificate of A level within 15 days of announcement of result or at the time of admission. I could not appear in NUST Entry Test, Can I take the Test on some other day?

Mdcat

NUST is conducting NET in paper based format on one day and in computer based format in two sessions daily, on different days, as per the schedule. As per policy, if a candidate misses the Test session he / she is not given another chance. But in case of extreme emergency, requests may be entertained on case to case basis, depending on availability of space. I have DAE background. Which programmes can I apply for? DAE candidate should have completed DAE with minimum 60% marks in Matric as well as DAE
The detail of Engineering programmes offered to DAE candidates as approved by Pakistan Engineering Council (PEC) can be accessed by visiting
https://nust.edu.pk/admissions/undergraduates/dae-eligibility-criteria/.

Mdcat

If I am selected on all 3 merits, what should I do? If selected on the basis of NET and ACT / SAT on national as well as international seat, you have the option to retain any one seat of your own choice. If I have taken ACT / SAT Tests, do I still have to sit in the Entry Test? Separate admission form will be available on NUST website for applying on the basis of
ACT / SAT
and
NET. Those applying on ACT / SAT basis does not require to appear in NET, however they can opt for NET in addition to ACT /SAT specified seats. In this way, they will be considered for both the streams.

Mdcat

Is a candidate having less than 60% marks in FSc part-1 eligible to apply for admission in NUST? Yes, the marks entered for FSc part-1 will be utilized for merit generation. However on joining NUST, the candidate must have secure 60% or more marks in FSc complete result. Is an equivalence certificate of qualification other than Matric & FSc / FA necessary? Can I not submit my O'level and A' level certificates? (I ask so, because I will be applying from abroad)? Equivalence certificates duly issued by IBCC Pakistan are necessary for those candidates who do not possess Matric & FSc / FA qualifications. Submission of only O&A level grade / mark sheets will not serve the purpose.

Mdcat

All non FSc stream candidates must have equivalence certificate of their qualification, duly obtained from IBCC, Pakistan in relevant groups/subjects and with minimum 60% marks. Is migration to NUST allowed? Yes, as per migration policy, students can apply for migration after completion of first year and before the beginning of final year. However, applicants with exceptionally good academic record are considered for migration to NUST. For detailed migration policy
click here. Is there any age limit for Undergraduate Admission? There is no age limit for Undergraduate Admission. Is there any installment plan for the payment of tuition fee?

Mdcat

As per PM&DC Regulations, a student can pay the tuition fee in installments on a
quarterly,
six monthly
or
annual basis, with a discount of
0%
on quarterly,
2%
on six monthly and
4%
on annual basis. Necessary details will be incorporated in the fee invoice. Is there any negative marking in the NUST Entry Test? No, there is no negative marking. Name on my CNIC and Passport is different from that on my academic documents (O / A Level) or equivalence certificate. Which name should I use to apply for admission?

Mdcat

For this, you have to get your name changed on your CNIC and Passport, because University will use the name as per your Matric or O level equivalence certificate (SSC), duly issued by IBCC. NSHS is affiliated to which University? NSHS is a constituent school of NUST. The MBBS degree will be awarded by NUST. Question: How frequently are University fee rates revised? University fees are usually announced once each year, for the following academic year. However, fees are subject to change without notice. What are the different Undergraduate Programmes offered at NUST? For information regarding offered UG Programmes,
click here. What are the salient features of UG Admissions at NUST? For salient features, please
click here.

Mdcat

What are the subjects for Entry Test for different categories of candidates? https://nust.edu.pk/admissions/undergraduates/subjects-included-in-net-with-weightings/
What category of candidates can apply for admission on the basis of ACT / SAT? Any candidate can apply for admission on the basis of ACT / SAT provided he / she has score for the prescribed disciplines of study. Separate admission form will be available on NUST website for this purpose. Admission on ACT / SAT basis has two categories, i.e National and International. Candidates admitted on National seats pay tuition fee and allied charges in Pak currency whereas candidates admitted against International seats pay the required dues at rates defined for international students.

Mdcat

For admission against international seats, the following categories will be eligible:-
Pakistani national students having passed an examination equivalent to intermediate level of Pakistan from a foreign education system. Foreigners and Pakistanis having dual nationality, irrespective of the place of their HSSC study, i.e Pakistan or abroad. Pakistani students who studied abroad but have passed the requisite examination from a Pakistani board. What documents are essentially required to be submitted while applying for admission? While applying for admission, possession of Computerized National Identity Card (CNIC) / Form 'B' or NICOP is mandatory. Online registration system does not require attachment of documents / credentials. However, the same will be required for verification during selection and admission process of selected candidates.

Mdcat

What is the admission criteria for a gap year / repeater candidates? Gap year / repeater candidates are being considered as normal candidates and there is no penalization while generating the merit. What is the admission criteria for BSHND programme? The detailed admission criteria is given on the following link:
https://nust.edu.pk/admissions/alliedprogram/admission-criteria/
What is the admission schedule for BSHND programme? https://nust.edu.pk/admissions/alliedprogram/dates-to-remember/
What is the application processing fee for online registration for admission? Application processing fee will be charged according to the following structure:
NUST Entry Test (per exam)
Pakistani / Dual Nationality Holder - Rs. 5,000/- or USD 40/-
Foreign National (Non Pakistani) - Rs.

Mdcat

10,000/- or USD 80/-
For ACT / SAT Candidates
ACT / SAT based National seat - Rs. 5,000/- or USD 40/-
ACT / SAT based International seat - Rs. 10,000/- or USD 80/-
Application processing fee is
neither refundable nor transferable. It will remain valid only for the series for which it has been paid. What is the deadline for submission of ACT / SAT score? Last date for ACT / SAT score to reach NUST directly from College Board, USA, is 25 July, 2025. NUST institutional code to receive ACT scores is 2915 and of SAT scores is 2790. ACT / SAT scores are valid for two years only.

Mdcat

It is the responsibility of candidates to ensure the provision of ACT / SAT scores to Undergraduate Section, NUST, Sector H-12, Islamabad by the given deadline. What is the detail of ACT / SAT Score required for admission? NUST requires ACT / SAT scores for admission in undergraduate programmes as per the following link:-
https://nust.edu.pk/admissions/undergraduates/applying-on-the-basis-of-scholastic-aptitude-test-sat/
What is the duration of test and the number of MCQs to be attempted? There are total
200 MCQs
to be attempted in
three hours
(180 minutes). What is the eligibility criteria for admission in different undergraduate programmes? https://nust.edu.pk/admissions/undergraduates/eligibility-criteria-for-ug-programme
s/
What is the fee structure for MBBS progamme in NSHS? Detailed fee structure for MBBS programme can be viewed by visiting
https://nust.edu.pk/admissions/NSHS/fee-structure/.

Mdcat

What is the fee structure for the progamme? Please visit:
https://nust.edu.pk/admissions/alliedprogram/fee-structure-bshnd/
What is the fee structure of different UG programmes? Please visit:
https://nust.edu.pk/admissions/fee-structure/undergraduate-financial-matters/
What is the Merit Generation Criteria for admission in MBBS Programme? https://nust.edu.pk/admissions/NSHS/merit-generation-criteria-mbbs/
What is the merit generation criteria for BSHND programme? Merit details are as under:
NET / MDCAT / ACT Test
*
- 75%
HSSC (Pre-Medical) / A level / Equivalent - 15%
SSC / O level / Equivalent - 10%
*
For ACT Based International Seats,
100% weightage
will be given to required ACT Score. What is the syllabus of Entry Test subjects and format of questions?

Mdcat

NUST Entry Test is primarily based on SSC / HSSC level studies (Part-1 & Part-II) and questions are in the form of MCQs with four answer options each. The paper of English is on the pattern of SAT exam. Sample Test. Textbooks are consulted while developing the question bank. Grasping the concepts available in different textbooks will be beneficial. https://nust.edu.pk/admissions/undergraduates/subjects-included-in-net-with-weightings
What is the test venue for NET in different cities? In
Islamabad, NET is conducted in NUST Exam Hall (Entry through Gate - 4) located inside the NUST Main Campus, Sector H-12, Islamabad. Pin location:
https://rb.gy/j0b5ds
In
Quetta, NET is conducted at NUST Balochistan Campus (NBC).

Mdcat

Pin Location:
https://rb.gy/9ntumc
In
Karachi, and
Gilgit, the test venue is mentioned on respective admit cards. What is the timeline for the announcement of NET result? Result of computer-based NET is uploaded within
24 hours
and for paper-based NET, within
5 to 7 working days. Result can be viewed by entering the Roll No. in the following link:
https://ugadmissions.nust.edu.pk/result/default.aspx
When will the admission for NSHS be advertised? Admissions will be announced in electronic and print media as per NUMS and PM&DC Policy. When will the classes for MBBS programme start? Classes for MBBS programme will commence as per PM&DC guidelines. Where is NSHS located? NSHS is located inside the NUST main campus, sector H-12, Islamabad.

Mdcat

The link to its pin location (on Google Maps) is:
https://rb.gy/f6a26
Where is NUST School of Health Sciences (NSHS) located? NSHS is located inside the NUST Main Campus, Sector H-12, Islamabad. The link to its pin location (on Google Maps) is:
https://rb.gy/f6a26
Which admission tests are acceptable for admission in BSHND programme? For Open Merit Seats
NET-Applied Sciences

Nums Mdcat

National MDCAT
For ACT Based Seats
ACT Test
Which MDCAT will be valid for admission in NSHS? You can apply for admission on the basis of
NUMS MDCAT only. Who is exempted from payment of NET Application Processing Fee? First, second and third position holders in HSSC exams of different BISEs are exempted from payment of NET Application Processing Fee. Such candidates should contact NUST through email at
[email protected]
along with valid proof obtained from the concerned education board. Whom should I contact for queries about NUST Entry Test for Undergraduate Programmes? You can contact through
Phone: +92 51-90856878
e-mail:
[email protected]. Will the admission be confirmed in case of quarterly or six-monthly payment of tuition fee?

Nums Mdcat

The student will be admitted to the MBBS programme on payment of tuition fee in quarterly or six-monthly installments. Will there be hostel facility available for the MBBS students? The facility will be available both for boys and girls as per the NUST SoP. The students are requested to apply for the same on confirmation of selection.

Nums Mdcat

For the details regarding hostel charges and to apply online, visit the following links:
https://campuslife.nust.edu.pk/facility-and-amenity/housing-and-dining/
Would the admission processing fee / security deposit be refundable in case a student does not join the University?
The university will refund only the security deposit to the candidates who do not join the university. Admission Processing Fee is non-refundable.
FAQs Categories
BSHND Admissions - FAQs
MBBS Admissions - FAQs
UG Admissions - FAQs

FREQUENTLY ASKED QUESTIONS (FAQS) | NUST (Scholarship FAQs)

Admissions
Scholarships
FREQUENTLY ASKED QUESTIONS (FAQS)
FREQUENTLY ASKED QUESTIONS (FAQS)
Q. How many types of financial aid are offered? There are three types of financial aid offered by NUST:
Need based scholarships. Merit based scholarships / financial assistance. Interest free loans. Q. When and How to Apply for Financial Aid? Under Graduate (UG) & Masters (MS) candidates apply for financial aid immediately after submitting online admission application. To apply candidates are required to submit online NUST need Based Financial Aid Application Form (NFAAF). Due date for submitting NFAAF is the same as that of the online admission application. Due date for MS & UG is tentatively May & June 2022 respectively. Please visit NUST admission portal for exact dates.

FREQUENTLY ASKED QUESTIONS (FAQS) | NUST (Scholarship FAQs)

Financial Aid Applications will not be accepted separately after due date. Q. Who can apply for financial aid? All UG & Masters candidate(s) are eligible to apply for financial aid at the time of admission. Q. Does applying for financial aid have any effect on admission application? OR
I have completed my admission application but I have not filled / partially filled the financial aid application and I am not interested to apply for financial aid. Will leaving the financial aid form unfilled / partially filled, effect my admission application? No, financial aid application has no effect on admission decision. Q. What is meant by physical verification?

FREQUENTLY ASKED QUESTIONS (FAQS) | NUST (Scholarship FAQs)

NUST team may visit your house, business premises or carry out inquiry from the surrounding neighbors to physically verify facts stated by you while applying for financial aid. Physical Verification may be carried out at any point of time during academic life cycle at NUST.
Q. I have submitted NFAAF and attached soft copies of supporting documents too, do I need to send hard copies of supporting documents? If yes, When?

FREQUENTLY ASKED QUESTIONS (FAQS) | NUST (Scholarship FAQs)

You do not need to send supporting documents to FAO immediately after submitting NFAAF. After due processing, FAO will inform you via email to mail the documents in hard form, within a due date. Failure to provide documents within due date will lead to cancellation of NFAAF. Q. I want to apply for other need based scholarships like PEEF, CMEEF KPK, FEF etc. Do I need to submit another application form? Upon submitting NFAAF, you will be considered for all types of need based scholarships. FAO will inform you incase any additional form / document is required. Q. I do not have few of the supporting documents. What should i do?

FREQUENTLY ASKED QUESTIONS (FAQS) | NUST (Scholarship FAQs)

OR
I need more time to arrange the supporting documents. Please allow me to submit NFAAF and provide supporting documents in a few more working days. You may attach scan copy of a hand written letter of explanation (duly signed by you and your parent / guardian). Briefly explain reason of not attaching the supporting document and expected date till you will provide the same to Financial Aid Office. Q. I am trying to upload soft copy of the supporting documents but the system is not saving them. What should i do? File size of each of the supporting document should be less than 250 kb. Resize the scan copy of supporting document and try uploading the same.

FREQUENTLY ASKED QUESTIONS (FAQS) | NUST (Scholarship FAQs)

If problem persists, you may forward a screen shot of the error to
[email protected]. Q. I have been offered admission in two or more disciplines and have NOT been offered scholarship in ALL of these disciplines. Is this provisional scholarship offer valid for only specific disciplines? Scholarship offered is valid for ALL disciplines offered admission in a particular selection list e.g if you secure admission in BS Physics, Chemistry & Maths in the 1st selection list, provisional admission letters wi ll have the same scholarship offer. If you have NOT been offered scholarship in any of the disciplines, please write to us at
[email protected]. Q.

FREQUENTLY ASKED QUESTIONS (FAQS) | NUST (Scholarship FAQs)

I have secured admission (for example in BS Civil Engineering in 1st selection list) and have been offered need based scholarship. Will this scholarship be transferred to other discipline(s) I am offered admission in subsequent selection list(s). How will the scholarship be transferred to other disciplines? What happens to the scholarship offer if I don t pay dues now and wait for securing admission in subsequent lists? Scholarship is awarded on merit cum need basis i.e on the basis of a candidates position on the financial need scale in a particular selection list.

FREQUENTLY ASKED QUESTIONS (FAQS) | NUST (Scholarship FAQs)

Scholarship once offered to you may be transferred to ALL other offered disciplines, only if you have already deposited dues to secure admission / scholarship i.e for BS Civil Engineering in 1st selection list in the example above. To transfer scholarship offer to other disciplines, you will be required to give willingness to admission office (
[email protected],
[email protected]
). Scholarship offered to you- for BS Civil Engg in the example above- will be cancelled incase you do not deposit dues, and vacant slot will be offered in the subsequent selection list on merit cum need basis. Q. My Financial Profile Has Changed After Submission Of NFAAF. How Can I Make Changes In NFAAF After Submission?

FREQUENTLY ASKED QUESTIONS (FAQS) | NUST (Scholarship FAQs)

OR I Have Made Mistakes In The NFAAF. I Cannot Access It After Submission. How Can I Get It Corrected? You will be required to inform FAO via email (
[email protected]
) specifically mentioning the following:
Section of NFAAF. Sub section of NFAAF. Title of the Field(s) to be amended. Amended Values. If necessary, someone from the FAO may contact you on contact numbers (provided in the NFAAF) to acquire additional information from you / family members.

FREQUENTLY ASKED QUESTIONS (FAQS) | NUST (Scholarship FAQs)

Q. What Happens If My Parents Are Divorced, Separated And One Parent Is Unwilling To Submit Any Information To The Financial Aid Office Or To Contribute Towards My NUST Expenses?

FREQUENTLY ASKED QUESTIONS (FAQS) | NUST (Scholarship FAQs)

In fairness to all, NUST expects that parents will pay what they can, and we must assess each parent s ability to contribute to college costs by reviewing complete financial documents from both parents. You might explain to the parent, unwilling to submit financial information, that his or her information will remain confidential. Your parent should also know that providing such information no way obligates him or her to contribute to your NUST education. If you believe your situation is special, please write us a detailed letter of explanation. Q. May I Send The Deed Of Agreement In The Same Envelope In Which I Am Sending Admission Documents? Deed of agreement is to be forwarded to Financial Aid Office separately.

FREQUENTLY ASKED QUESTIONS (FAQS) | NUST (Scholarship FAQs)

Q. What Is The Need Assessment Process? The decision and its review follow the stages outlined below:
The staff at the financial aid office (FAO) checks and reconciles the information on the financial aid form against the documents provided including their tenor and validity; seeking clarifications from the applicant/ student on anomalies, if any. Applicants are contacted for retrieval of discrepant documents (where necessary). FAO prepares student evaluation sheets that contain a brief summary of the credentials provided covering all the aspects to be presented to the financial aid committee for final decision. Shortlisted Candidates are informed to submit supporting documentation in hard form by the deadline specified by the financial aid office.

FREQUENTLY ASKED QUESTIONS (FAQS) | NUST (Scholarship FAQs)

Interviews / Physical Verifications are conducted where recommended by the committee. The NUST financial aid committee assesses each case through the evaluation sheet and reviews supporting documents before making their decision which is purely based on need. Decision is communicated to the students through an official provisional scholarship award letter. The Financial Aid Office reserves the right to conduct periodic reviews to ensure the award is granted to the needy students only. Q. My Father / Guardian / Earning Member Of The Family Does Not Have A Job. What Type Of Income Certificate Should I Give?

FREQUENTLY ASKED QUESTIONS (FAQS) | NUST (Scholarship FAQs)

Father / Guardian / Earning Member(s) of the Family will give a Declaration of monthly income from all sources on affidavit to be printed on stamp paper having value > Rs.100, duly attested by Union Councilor & two witnesses. At least one of the witnesses is to be a Govt. officer of BPS 17 or above & the other a close family member well acquainted with your family financial profile. Contact numbers of the witnesses to be mentioned on the affidavit. Q. My Father / Guardian Does Business. What Type Of Income Certificate Should I Give?

FREQUENTLY ASKED QUESTIONS (FAQS) | NUST (Scholarship FAQs)

Father / Guardian / Earning Member(s) of the Family will give a Declaration of monthly income from all sources (business) on affidavit to be printed on stamp paper having value > Rs.100, duly attested by Union Councilor & two witnesses. At least one of the witnesses is to be a Govt. officer of BPS 17 or above & the other a close family member well acquainted with your family financial profile. Contact numbers of the witnesses to be mentioned on the affidavit. Q. My Father / Guardian Does Job And Has A Part Time Business (Agriculture, Taxi, Tuition, Shop Etc). What Type Of Income Certificate Should I Give?

FREQUENTLY ASKED QUESTIONS (FAQS) | NUST (Scholarship FAQs)

Father / Guardian / Earning Member(s) of the Family will give salary certificate and a Declaration of monthly income from all sources (job & Business) on affidavit to be printed on stamp paper having value > Rs.100, duly attested by Union Councilor & two witnesses. At least one of the witnesses is to be a Govt. officer of BPS 17 or above & the other a close family member well acquainted with your family financial profile. Contact numbers of the witnesses to be mentioned on the affidavit. Q. Our Monthly Income Is From Agricultural Lands. What Type Of Income Certificate Should I Give?

FREQUENTLY ASKED QUESTIONS (FAQS) | NUST (Scholarship FAQs)

Father / guardian will a Declaration of monthly income from all sources on affidavit to be printed on stamp paper having value > Rs.100, duly attested by Assistant Commissioner Revenue / Union Councilor & two witnesses. At least one of the witnesses is to be a Govt. officer of BPS 17 or above & the other a close family member well acquainted with your family financial profile. Contact numbers of the witnesses to be mentioned on the affidavit. Q. I Was Awarded A Scholarship From A Private Outside Organization. Do I Need To Inform Financial Aid Office?

FREQUENTLY ASKED QUESTIONS (FAQS) | NUST (Scholarship FAQs)

Yes, if you receive an outside grant or scholarship or a third-party payment you must report the type and amount to the FAO as and when received. Q. Can I Apply F Or The Financial Aid During The Semester/Year? Only those students, whose financial situation changes drastically, due to unforeseen circumstances e.g. Retirement, death, layoffs from job etc. can apply for financial assistance during the semester/ year through their institutions. Q. What Is The Criteria Of Awarding Financial Aid? The scholarship management committee assesses financial need of all candidates and determines whether or not candidate s family has sufficient resources to afford fee at NUST. The need is determined on the basis of the financial credentials submitted by the candidate.

FREQUENTLY ASKED QUESTIONS (FAQS) | NUST (Scholarship FAQs)

Q. Can a student appeal for review of the financial assistance decision? There is an appeal process in place in which the NUST administration accepts requests from students who have additional information to declare, which may have been missed during the initial assessment. Undergraduate Programmes
How to Apply for NET

Financial Assistance For Undergraduate Students

FREQUENTLY ASKED QUESTIONS (FAQS) GUIDELINES TO SUBMIT NEED BASED FINANCIAL AID APPLICATION FORM (NFAAF) INTEREST FREE LOAN (IHSAN TRUST) MERIT BASED SCHOLARSHIPS FOR MASTERS AND PhD

Marcoms-Prospectus-2025-V.5.0-04032025 Compressed - Page 2

This prospectus contains basic information about the undergraduate, masters, and PhD programmes, life at the University, learning resources and advice to local and international students who wish to join the University in Fall 2025. It also shows the fee structure, funding modes (financial assistance), procedure for applying for admission and criteria for entry. About The University

National University of Sciences and Technology (NUST) was established in March 1991 for promotion of higher scientific education in the country, especially in the fields of science and technology by providing stable and disciplined academic environment together with need-based research, pertinent to industrial requirements. The University was granted its Charter in 1993. Over the years, the university has expanded in scope, services and stature and has emerged as a leading comprehensive University in the public sector. Within two decades, NUST has achieved important milestones and gained immense significance as an institution of higher education in Pakistan. The University produces professionals and researchers of very high calibre, capable of developing indigenous technologies to meet the growing demands of the 21st century.

NUST Business School (NBS)
Launched in 1999 as NUST Institute of Management Sciences and rebranded as NUST Business School (NBS) in 2008, the School
is situated in the vast central NUST campus in Sector H-12 Islamabad. It is highly reputed within Pakistan and has made an
international footprint in recent years. As a testament of the quality of education it imparts and the standing of its graduates in the
market, it has consistently featured at #2 in Pakistan in the QS Subject Wise Rankings since 2019 and was ranked in the 251-300
bracket in the 2023 QS World Rankings.

Marcoms-Prospectus-2025-V.5.0-04032025 Compressed - Page 26

It also enjoys full accreditation (5 years) of the three programs (BBA, BS AC&F and MBA)
which fall under the purview of the National Business Education Accreditation Council (NBEAC). The program portfolio consists of
three undergraduate, two taught masters (MBA and EMBA), four MS (HRM, Logistics & Supply Chain Management, Innovation and
Entrepreneurship and Marketing) and one PhD program. This planned growth in programs was accompanied by an increase in the
student body from of 97 in 1999 to the current approximate strength of 1250 in 2023. There are approximately 3500 NBS alumni
as well who are serving the industry, academia and society both, in Pakistan and abroad.

Marcoms-Prospectus-2025-V.5.0-04032025 Compressed - Page 26

a formidable reputation in a short span of over two decades. In the quest to extend this reputation beyond our borders, we have

(AACSB) represents the highest standard of achievement for business schools worldwide and is a hallmark of excellence in
management education. Institutions that earn the AACSB accreditation, confirm their commitment to quality and continuous
improvement.

School of Social Sciences and Humanities (S3H)
The discipline of social sciences plays a vital role in understanding the function of society, studying individual behavior and evaluating
social problems and their impact on society. Taking cognizance of the significance of social sciences and humanities discipline,
NUST has recently established the School of Social Sciences and Humanities (S3H). Founded in 2013, the history of the school can
be traced back to 1999 when its seed began to germinate in the form of establishment of NUST Institute of Management Sciences
(NIMS), which was renamed as NUST Business School (NBS) in 2008.

Marcoms-Prospectus-2025-V.5.0-04032025 Compressed - Page 27

Later, three social sciences departments namely Department
of Economics, Department of Government and Public Policy, and Department of Mass Communication were launched and initially
housed under the umbrella of NUST Business School. In 2013, the university felt the need to have a proper institution devoted to social sciences, so five departments; Economics; Mass
Communication; Behavioral Sciences; Government and Public Policy and Development Studies – were combined to form S3H. Along with that, the school also has Chinese Studies Center and Center of Excellence on Population and Wellbeing Studies (CePwS)
working under its banner.

Marcoms-Prospectus-2025-V.5.0-04032025 Compressed - Page 27

The school relocated to its own new, state-of-the-art building in 2015 and since then is committed to
high- quality undergraduate and graduate instruction across the social sciences and humanities. The school has established a Law
department in 2020. Our interdisciplinary faculty areas of expertise include economics, psychology, career counselling, environmental and energy
policy, digital media, journalism, international relations, development studies, law and linguistics. We pride ourselves on providing students with the opportunity to engage in hands-on educational experiences and apply academic
concepts, strategies and techniques practically to contemporary real-world issues. In less than a decade, S3H has made its mark as one of the premier schools of social sciences in Pakistan and a training ground par
excellence for future leaders.

School of Interdisciplinary Engineering and Sciences (SINES)
School of Interdisciplinary Engineering & Sciences (SINES), a flagship School of NUST, was established in February 2022 to promote
and cultivate interdisciplinary education & research and respond to the contemporary challenges that require cross-cutting
knowledge & expertise from multiple domains. Its legacy dates to 2007, when it operated as a post-graduate Research Centre for
Modelling & Simulation (RCMS). SINES is implementing its vision through three mechanisms. Firstly, it offers interdisciplinary &
multi-school academic programs through its unique and flexible academic structure, enabling excellence of instruction and efficient
resource utilization.

Marcoms-Prospectus-2025-V.5.0-04032025 Compressed - Page 34

Secondly, to promote interdisciplinary research, 30 high-end research labs from 11 NUST Schools have been
co-located under one roof and working in diverse domains, including AI, Robotics, IoT, Chip Design, Biotechnology, Bioinformatics,
Cyber Security, Radar Technology, Renewable Energy, AR-VR and more. These labs are clustered into five SDG-inspired themes,
namely i. Energy and Environment, ii. Health and Well-Being, iii. Agriculture and Food Security, iv. Sensors and Automation and v. Materials and Manufacturing. The labs are supported by a central High-Performance-Computer (HPC) clocking at 1000-Teraflops,
making it the fastest computer in the academic sector of Pakistan.

Marcoms-Prospectus-2025-V.5.0-04032025 Compressed - Page 34

design centres and labs that can provide engagement avenues with the relevant research labs and its students & faculty. As a

local companies including RapidsAI, Resolve, Ghandara Dynamics have been established. In collaboration with Jazz, SINES also has
the privilege to host country s only 5G Innovation Lab housing end-to-end 5G network infrastructure for R&D purpose. Lastly, a well

The Atta-ur-Rahman School of Applied Biosciences (ASAB) was founded in October 2007 as the NUST Centre of Virology and

renamed in honor of Prof. Dr. Atta-ur-Rahman, a highly distinguished scientist from Pakistan who has received several prestigious

programs that equip students with the skills and knowledge necessary for research and teaching in both pure molecular and
applied biology.

Phytochemistry, Molecular Phylogenetics, Cell Signaling, Regenerative Medicine, and Agribusiness Management. Our faculty

Pakistan Navy Engineering College (PNEC)
The genesis of PN Engineering College lies in the Officers Training Section (OTS) which was set up in 1962. In 1966, the status of
OTS was upgraded to that of a college and given the name of Pakistan Navy Engineering College (PNEC). PNEC was affiliated with
Karachi University in the same year. In 1977, the affiliation of PNEC was transferred to NED University of Engineering & Technology. The college was shifted to its present location in 1982. In 1995 it became constituent college of National University of Sciences
and Technology (NUST). PNEC Offering comprehensive undergraduate degrees in Electrical Engineering, Mechanical Engineering,
Maritime Sciences (for PN officers only) and Naval Architecture.

and offers postgraduate degrees in Electrical Engineering, Mechanical Engineering, Industrial & Manufacturing Engineering, Naval

The Financial Aid Program of NUST aims to enable students from across all strata of society, particularly those belonging
to financially challenged family backgrounds thus reducing inequalities while creating educational equity. The need-driven
program is one of the most robust and effective in the country providing financial aid in the form of need-based scholarships,
loans, on-campus jobs, etc. In addition, students excelling in their respective degree programs also get merit-based scholarships.

Marcoms-Prospectus-2025-V.5.0-04032025 Compressed - Page 75

Financial aid is provided through a competitive selection process which comprises documentation, committee
interviews, and physical ground checks. Maximum efforts are undertaken to ensure that the most financially
challenged students are awarded financial aid on a sliding scale. Scholarships are awarded based on financial need
and they cover a range of academic expenditures ranging from partial tuition fees to full tuition fees and more. Other Opportunities for Financial Assistance
Government and other philanthropic organizations or individuals provide scholarships to financially challenged students studying at
NUST. Students are informed of these opportunities through the NUST portal (URL: https://nust.edu.pk/admissions/scholarships/)
and respective institutions.

Marcoms-Prospectus-2025-V.5.0-04032025 Compressed - Page 75

UG Admissions - FAQs | NUST (Category Page)

Frequently Asked Questions (UG Admissions - FAQs) Can a candidate of ICS / equivalent qualification eligible to apply for Engineering programmes? Yes, as per PEC regulation No PEC/EAD/46-VCCM/Regulations/2023 dated 30 March 2023, candidates having combination of Physics, Mathematics and Computer Studies / Computer Science (ICS) or equivalent are eligible to apply for all the Engineering programs offered by NUST, but they have to clear Chemistry as a remedial subject / course in the 1st Semester after admission. Candidates, who already appeared in any NET series during current admission cycle, will also be able to update their choice preferences while applying for Series-4 or after the announcement of merit, before start of the selection process.

UG Admissions - FAQs | NUST (Category Page)

Can a candidate with FSc Pre-Medical / equivalent qualification, appeared in additional mathematics paper and waiting for the result, apply for admission in Engineering programs on the basis of Pre Medical marks? Candidate can apply on the basis of Pre-Medical result, however he / she has to provide the additional math result with passing marks and overall 60% marks in HSSC at the time of joining NUST. Can candidates of FSc Pre-Medical / equivalent qualification apply for BS Computer Science? Yes, but they have to clear deficient Mathematics courses of 6 credit hours, within one year of enrollment in the programme. Can I apply for computing programmes with HSSC / A level background in Arts / Humanities group?

UG Admissions - FAQs | NUST (Category Page)

Can I apply for computing programmes with HSSC / A level background in Arts / Humanities group? Yes, any candidate who has Mathematics as one of the mandatory subjects in HSSC / A level / Equivalent, is eligible to apply for computing programmes. Can I apply for more than one programme? Yes, if you meet the eligibility criteria. Can I apply for rechecking of Entry Test Result? Re-checking of Paper Based Entry Test may be requested within 5 days of the declaration of result along with a fee of Rs.500/- in the form of a bank draft/pay order in favour of NUST. The original bank draft / pay order should be dispatched

UG Admissions - FAQs | NUST (Category Page)

dispatched to Admissions Directorate, NUST, Sector H-12, Islamabad, along with written application. Re-checking involves the verification of paper for any unmarked answers and error free totaling of marks. Can I get a copy of NUST Entry Test result? Desirous candidates may obtain the result details of their Test from the link available on NUST website. Can I get some sample Test papers of NUST Entry Test? Mock Test for Computer Based / Paper Based Test are available on NUST website www.ugadmissions.nust.edu.pk under the link Sample Test, for candidates to practice. Do I fall under the Expatriate Students category?

UG Admissions - FAQs | NUST (Category Page)

Do I fall under the Expatriate Students category? If you are temporarily or permanently residing in a country other than Pakistan, and hold either a Pakistani passport, or dual nationality (one of them being Pakistani), you fall under the Expatriate Student category. For details, please click here. Does NUST offer scholarship / financial assistance? Information regarding scholarships offered by NUST can be accessed by clicking here. Students showing good academic performance by maintaining a minimum GPA of 3.5 out of 4.0 in each semester, are eligible to get financial assistance, as per policy. For various academics backgrounds, which options for various UG disciplines are available?

UG Admissions - FAQs | NUST (Category Page)

For various academics backgrounds, which options for various UG disciplines are available? The options available to candidates for admission in various UG programmes according to their academic background are accessible by visiting following link:- https://nust.edu.pk/admissions/undergraduates/academic-qualification-required-for-different-ug-progammes/ How can I submit the application processing fee (online) using 1Link option? Candidates can use the unique 17-digit 1Bill Invoice / Consumer No. mentioned under the Fee Invoice section, to pay their fee via 1Link service through any of the following payment modes: Mobile App / Online Banking (Any Bank) ATM (Any Bank) Easy Paisa / Jazz Cash etc. The benefit of making payment through 1Link is that you don t have to wait for fee verification because it is verified instantly.

UG Admissions - FAQs | NUST (Category Page)

The benefit of making payment through 1Link is that you don t have to wait for fee verification because it is verified instantly. How can I submit the application processing fee? Candidates can submit the fee through any of the following options:- 1Link (1Bill Invoice Number) Credit Card I am a foreigner and want to get admission at NUST? If you fulfill the eligibility criteria, you can apply for admission on the basis of NET or ACT / SAT (International seat) or both as per policy in vogue. For details, visit https://nust.edu.pk/admissions/undergraduates/international-students/.

UG Admissions - FAQs | NUST (Category Page)

https://nust.edu.pk/admissions/undergraduates/international-students/. I am an overseas Pakistani who has completed O & A level from abroad, do I have to pass Urdu, Islamiyat / Ethics, and Pakistan Studies to get IBCC equivalence? No, there isn t any such compulsory requirement of passing the above-mentioned subjects, as per IBCC equivalence rules. For further details, please visit IBCC website: https://ibcc.edu.pk/rules-for-equivalence/ I am in my final year of A Level and my result will be announced in August 2024. Can I apply?

UG Admissions - FAQs | NUST (Category Page)

I am in my final year of A Level and my result will be announced in August 2024. Can I apply? Yes, you can apply, as in such case you will be evaluated on the basis of O levels equivalence marks but you have to provide equivalence certificate of A level within 15 days of announcement of result or at the time of admission. I could not appear in NUST Entry Test, Can I take the Test on some other day?

UG Admissions - FAQs | NUST (Category Page)

I could not appear in NUST Entry Test, Can I take the Test on some other day? NUST is conducting NET in paper based format on one day and in computer based format in two sessions daily, on different days, as per the schedule. As per policy, if a candidate misses the Test session he / she is not given another chance. But in case of extreme emergency, requests may be entertained on case to case basis, depending on availability of space. I have DAE background. Which programmes can I apply for? DAE candidate should have completed DAE with minimum 60% marks in Matric as well as DAE

UG Admissions - FAQs | NUST (Category Page)

DAE candidate should have completed DAE with minimum 60% marks in Matric as well as DAE The detail of Engineering programmes offered to DAE candidates as approved by Pakistan Engineering Council (PEC) can be accessed by visiting https://nust.edu.pk/admissions/undergraduates/dae-eligibility-criteria/. If I am selected on all 3 merits, what should I do? If selected on the basis of NET and ACT / SAT on national as well as international seat, you have the option to retain any one seat of your own choice. If I have taken ACT / SAT Tests, do I still have to sit in the Entry Test? Separate admission form will be available on NUST website for applying on the basis of ACT / SAT and NET.

UG Admissions - FAQs | NUST (Category Page)

NET. Those applying on ACT / SAT basis does not require to appear in NET, however they can opt for NET in addition to ACT /SAT specified seats. In this way, they will be considered for both the streams. Is a candidate having less than 60% marks in FSc part-1 eligible to apply for admission in NUST? Yes, the marks entered for FSc part-1 will be utilized for merit generation. However on joining NUST, the candidate must have secure 60% or more marks in FSc complete result.

UG Admissions - FAQs | NUST (Category Page)

Is an equivalence certificate of qualification other than Matric & FSc / FA necessary? Can I not submit my O'level and A' level certificates? (I ask so, because I will be applying from abroad)? Equivalence certificates duly issued by IBCC Pakistan are necessary for those candidates who do not possess Matric & FSc / FA qualifications. Submission of only O&A level grade / mark sheets will not serve the purpose. All non FSc stream candidates must have equivalence certificate of their qualification, duly obtained from IBCC, Pakistan in relevant groups/subjects and with minimum 60% marks. Is migration to NUST allowed?

UG Admissions - FAQs | NUST (Category Page)

Is migration to NUST allowed? Yes, as per migration policy, students can apply for migration after completion of first year and before the beginning of final year. However, applicants with exceptionally good academic record are considered for migration to NUST. For detailed migration policy click here. Is there any age limit for Undergraduate Admission? There is no age limit for Undergraduate Admission. Is there any negative marking in the NUST Entry Test? No, there is no negative marking. Name on my CNIC and Passport is different from that on my academic documents (O / A Level) or equivalence certificate. Which name should I use to apply for admission?

UG Admissions - FAQs | NUST (Category Page)

For this, you have to get your name changed on your CNIC and Passport, because University will use the name as per your Matric or O level equivalence certificate (SSC), duly issued by IBCC. What are the different Undergraduate Programmes offered at NUST? For information regarding offered UG Programmes, click here. What are the salient features of UG Admissions at NUST? For salient features, please click here. What are the subjects for Entry Test for different categories of candidates? https://nust.edu.pk/admissions/undergraduates/subjects-included-in-net-with-weightings/ What category of candidates can apply for admission on the basis of ACT / SAT?

UG Admissions - FAQs | NUST (Category Page)

What category of candidates can apply for admission on the basis of ACT / SAT? Any candidate can apply for admission on the basis of ACT / SAT provided he / she has score for the prescribed disciplines of study. Separate admission form will be available on NUST website for this purpose. Admission on ACT / SAT basis has two categories, i.e National and International. Candidates admitted on National seats pay tuition fee and allied charges in Pak currency whereas candidates admitted against International seats pay the required dues at rates defined for international students. For admission against international seats, the following categories will be eligible:-

UG Admissions - FAQs | NUST (Category Page)

Pakistani national students having passed an examination equivalent to intermediate level of Pakistan from a foreign education system. Foreigners and Pakistanis having dual nationality, irrespective of the place of their HSSC study, i.e Pakistan or abroad. Pakistani students who studied abroad but have passed the requisite examination from a Pakistani board. What documents are essentially required to be submitted while applying for admission? While applying for admission, possession of Computerized National Identity Card (CNIC) / Form 'B' or NICOP is mandatory. Online registration system does not require attachment of documents / credentials. However, the same will be required for verification during selection and admission process of selected candidates. What is the admission criteria for a gap year / repeater candidates?

UG Admissions - FAQs | NUST (Category Page)

What is the admission criteria for a gap year / repeater candidates? Gap year / repeater candidates are being considered as normal candidates and there is no penalization while generating the merit. What is the application processing fee for online registration for admission? Application processing fee will be charged according to the following structure: NUST Entry Test (per exam) Pakistani / Dual Nationality Holder - Rs. 5,000/- or USD 40/- Foreign National (Non Pakistani) - Rs. 10,000/- or USD 80/- For ACT / SAT Candidates ACT / SAT based National seat - Rs. 5,000/- or USD 40/- ACT / SAT based International seat - Rs. 10,000/- or USD 80/- Application processing fee is

UG Admissions - FAQs | NUST (Category Page)

Application processing fee is neither refundable nor transferable. It will remain valid only for the series for which it has been paid. What is the deadline for submission of ACT / SAT score? Last date for ACT / SAT score to reach NUST directly from College Board, USA, is 25 July, 2025. NUST institutional code to receive ACT scores is 2915 and of SAT scores is 2790. ACT / SAT scores are valid for two years only. It is the responsibility of candidates to ensure the provision of ACT / SAT scores to Undergraduate Section, NUST, Sector H-12, Islamabad by the given deadline. What is the detail of ACT / SAT Score required for admission?

UG Admissions - FAQs | NUST (Category Page)

What is the detail of ACT / SAT Score required for admission? NUST requires ACT / SAT scores for admission in undergraduate programmes as per the following link:- https://nust.edu.pk/admissions/undergraduates/applying-on-the-basis-of-scholastic-aptitude-test-sat/ What is the duration of test and the number of MCQs to be attempted? There are total 200 MCQs to be attempted in three hours (180 minutes). What is the eligibility criteria for admission in different undergraduate programmes? https://nust.edu.pk/admissions/undergraduates/eligibility-criteria-for-ug-programme s/ What is the fee structure of different UG programmes? Please visit: https://nust.edu.pk/admissions/fee-structure/undergraduate-financial-matters/ What is the syllabus of Entry Test subjects and format of questions?

UG Admissions - FAQs | NUST (Category Page)

What is the syllabus of Entry Test subjects and format of questions? NUST Entry Test is primarily based on SSC / HSSC level studies (Part-1 & Part-II) and questions are in the form of MCQs with four answer options each. The paper of English is on the pattern of SAT exam. Sample Test. Textbooks are consulted while developing the question bank. Grasping the concepts available in different textbooks will be beneficial. https://nust.edu.pk/admissions/undergraduates/subjects-included-in-net-with-weightings What is the test venue for NET in different cities? In Islamabad, NET is conducted in NUST Exam Hall (Entry through Gate - 4) located inside the NUST Main Campus, Sector H-12, Islamabad. Pin location: https://rb.gy/j0b5ds In Quetta, NET is conducted at NUST Balochistan Campus (NBC). Pin Location:

UG Admissions - FAQs | NUST (Category Page)

Quetta, NET is conducted at NUST Balochistan Campus (NBC). Pin Location: https://rb.gy/9ntumc In Karachi, and Gilgit, the test venue is mentioned on respective admit cards. What is the timeline for the announcement of NET result? Result of computer-based NET is uploaded within 24 hours and for paper-based NET, within 5 to 7 working days. Result can be viewed by entering the Roll No. in the following link: https://ugadmissions.nust.edu.pk/result/default.aspx Who is exempted from payment of NET Application Processing Fee? First, second and third position holders in HSSC exams of different BISEs are exempted from payment of NET Application Processing Fee. Such candidates should contact NUST through email at [email protected] along with valid proof obtained from the concerned education board.

UG Admissions - FAQs | NUST (Category Page)

along with valid proof obtained from the concerned education board. Whom should I contact for queries about NUST Entry Test for Undergraduate Programmes? You can contact through Phone: +92 51-90856878 e-mail: [email protected]. Would the admission processing fee / security deposit be refundable in case a student does not join the University? The university will refund only the security deposit to the candidates who do not join the university. Admission Processing Fee is non-refundable.
